rulings.Case Title: THOMAS MCALLISTER et al VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 28, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Sears, Roebuck and Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs' claims are barred by the statute of limitations. Defendant demonstrated that Mr. McAllister was disabled in 2005, that his disability was at least contributed to by his exposure to asbestos and that he knew, or through the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known, the causes or contributing factors of his disability more than one year before plaintiffs filed this action. (Uram v. Abex Corp. (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425.) Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether their claims are barred by the statute of limitations. (Uram v. Abex Corp. (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425; Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103.) This ruling makes the other issues raised by defendant's motion moot. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS MCALLISTER et al VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT As To Statute Of Limitations
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 28, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs' claims are barred by the statute of limitations. Defendant demonstrated that Mr. McAllister was disabled in 2005, that his disability was at least contributed to by his exposure to asbestos and that he knew, or through the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known, the causes or contributing factors of his disability more than one year before plaintiffs filed this action. (Uram v. Abex Corp. (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425.) Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether their claims are barred by the statute of limitations. (Uram v. Abex Corp. (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425; Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103.) Plaintiffs' objections to the Vocational Feasibility Evaluation (Exhibit 4) and to the Wick declaration, paragraph 6, are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
